subject: fix(db): accept spatial_ref_sys RLS lint warning; remove unapplyable migration
body:
Tested live: Studio SQL Editor fails with the same 42501 as the
management API and `supabase db push`. I was wrong earlier in this
session when I claimed Studio was the escalation path — on this
project it runs as `postgres`, same as the others, and `postgres` is
not a member of `supabase_admin` so `SET ROLE` doesn't help.

Checked the full escalation surface before giving up:
- No shipped SECURITY DEFINER helper owned by supabase_admin covers
  RLS/ALTER (searched pg_proc for %rls%, %spatial%, %alter%, %admin%,
  %privileg% — zero matches).
- supabase_privileged_role isn't a member of supabase_admin and
  doesn't have UPDATE on spatial_ref_sys.

The only real path to flip the bit is a Supabase support ticket.
Not worth it — spatial_ref_sys is PostGIS public reference data, our
app never writes to it, and "writes denied by RLS" is already
enforced by table ownership (we can't write to a table we don't own).
The security value of enabling RLS here is nil.

Changes:
- Deleted supabase/migrations/20260409000001_enable_rls_spatial_ref_sys.sql
  so it doesn't sit permanently unapplied and mislead new sessions.
- CLAUDE.md: project-status line updated (17/17 applied + 1 accepted);
  AI Notes entry corrected to reflect that Studio does NOT escalate
  either; new "Accepted lint warnings" section documents the decision.
- docs/USER_ACTIONS.md: removed the now-obsolete "Enable RLS on
  spatial_ref_sys" section.
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md + docs/AI_MISTAKES.md: updated to record the
  correct outcome and the mistaken Studio-privileges assumption.

Tags: database, migrations, supabase, documentation
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 2
Impact: infrastructure
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: db-sync
Semver: patch
title: accept spatial_ref_sys RLS lint warning; remove unapplyable migration
